title: Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jammu & Kashmir

heroImage.alt: Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary, Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India.
overview.description: Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India. Situated in the Zanskar range near Sonamarg, the sanctuary spans approximately 210 km² and ranges in altitude from 3,015 to 5,466 meters. The terrain features rugged cliffs, alpine meadows, and the prominent Thajwas Glacier. The sanctuary forms a wildlife corridor connecting with Dachigam National Park and Overa-Aru Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Baltal, Jammu and Kashmir
topPlaces.description: Baltal is a camping ground for pilgrims, 75 km from Ganderbal town in Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India and 93 km from capital city Srinagar and 15 km north of Sonamarg on the Sind River at the base of Zojila pass. This little valley is only a day's journey away from Srinagar and provides a shorter high-altitude alternate route to the sacred cave of Amarnath.

topPlaces.name: Mount Kolahoi
topPlaces.description: Kolahoi Peak (locally called 'Gashe-braed' meaning Illuminated Cat) is a mountain with peak elevation of 5,425 metres (17,799 ft) located in Lidder Valley, Jammu and Kashmir. Kolahoi Peak is easily accessible through Aru Pahalgam.

topPlaces.name: Sirbal Peak
topPlaces.description: Sirbal Peak is a mountain with a peak elevation of 5,235 metres (17,175 ft), in the Ganderbal district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, in the vicinity of Sonamarg. Sirbal Peak is part of the Himalaya Range, and is located between Sonamarg and Baltal.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Baltal, Jammu and Kashmir & around

items.note: Baltal is a camping ground for pilgrims, 75 km from Ganderbal town in Ganderbal district of Jammu and Kashmir, India and 93 km from capital city Srinagar and 15 km north of Sonamarg on the Sind River at the base of Zojila pass. This little valley is only a day's journey away from Srinagar and provides a shorter high-altitude alternate route to the sacred cave of Amarnath.

Kolahoi Peak (locally called 'Gashe-braed' meaning Illuminated Cat) is a mountain with peak elevation of 5,425 metres (17,799 ft) located in Lidder Valley, Jammu and Kashmir. Kolahoi Peak is easily accessible through Aru Pahalgam.

Sirbal Peak is a mountain with a peak elevation of 5,235 metres (17,175 ft), in the Ganderbal district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir, in the vicinity of Sonamarg. Sirbal Peak is part of the Himalaya Range, and is located between Sonamarg and Baltal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sheikh ul-Alam International Airport, Srinagar (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (144 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baltal Thajwas Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Baltal, Jammu and Kashmir, Mount Kolahoi, Sirbal Peak.
